name: Abbaye de Saint-Sever-de-Rustan – loop in the valley of l'Arros
name_original: Abbaye de Saint-Sever-de-Rustan – boucle dans la vallée de l'Arros
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villecomtal-Sur-Arros features routes that traverse river valleys, agricultural lands, and wooded areas. The terrain generally consists of gentle rolling hills and flat sections, characteristic of the Gers department in southwestern France. Routes often follow the Arros and Adour rivers, providing varied scenery for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Villecomtal-Sur-Arros The most popular touring cycling route is Abbaye de Saint-Sever-de-Rustan – loop in the valley of l'Arros, an 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the scenic Arros river valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lac de Bours – View of the Adour loop from Rabastens-de-Bigorre, a difficult 24.7 miles (39.7 km) path. This route offers views of Lac de Bours and follows sections of the Adour river. Local touring cyclists also love the Joli Bois loop from Haget, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail leading through pleasant wooded areas, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Villecomtal-Sur-Arros is defined by river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and wooded sections. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Villecomtal-Sur-Arros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villecomtal-Sur-Arros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes around Villecomtal-Sur-Arros, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: The terrain generally consists of gentle rolling hills and flat sections, characteristic of the Gers department in southwestern France. Routes often follow the Arros and Adour rivers, traversing river valleys, agricultural lands, and wooded areas, providing varied scenery.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: Yes, there are 11 easy touring cycling routes available. An excellent option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021120656/abbaye-de-saint-sever-de-rustan-loop-in-the-valley-of-l-arros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Saint-Sever-de-Rustan – loop in the valley of l'Arros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.5 miles (18.5 km) long and explores the scenic Arros river valley.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes around Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: Yes, there are 12 difficult touring cycling routes for more experienced cyclists. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15013574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Bours – View of the Adour loop from Rabastens-de-Bigorre&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.7 miles (39.7 km) path offering views of Lac de Bours and following sections of the Adour river.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many routes in the region are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24664180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joli Bois loop from Haget&lt;/a&gt; is a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail that takes you through pleasant wooded areas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38655272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brambles loop from Villecomtal-sur-Arros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.5 miles (34.6 km) long.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or places to explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through picturesque landscapes. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever-de-Rustan Abbey&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Citadel Gate of Villecomtal-sur-Arros&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5860144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabournieu Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; also offers a tranquil spot for a break.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: The touring cycling routes in Villecomtal-Sur-Arros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and wooded sections.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: Yes, with 11 easy routes, there are several options suitable for families. These routes typically feature gentle gradients and scenic paths through the countryside, allowing for a relaxed cycling experience for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in this region of southwestern France, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring cooler, wetter weather.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring bike routes near Villecomtal-Sur-Arros?
A: While many routes are day trips, some, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15013574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Bours – View of the Adour loop from Rabastens-de-Bigorre&lt;/a&gt; at nearly 40 km, offer a longer ride. The network of routes allows for combining sections to create extended tours through the Gers department.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through wooded areas?
A: Yes, several routes lead through pleasant wooded sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24664180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joli Bois loop from Haget&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a refreshing ride through forest landscapes.
tips: Unique access door to the old citadel of Villecomtal, which was then fortified and no longer exists today. The ramparts have disappeared, the Porte d'Arcourten is the only vestige.
Protected as historical monuments, the Abbey, founded around the year 800, is nicknamed the "little Versailles of Bigorre". Visits possible between June and September.
